keyPoints: Create a new project in Android Studio: Right-click on the Android project directory and select 'New' > 'New Project'.
Choose the project location and save the project: Enter the project name, package name, and choose the project location.
Set up the directory structure: Create a new directory called 'res' for resources, 'layout' for user interfaces, 'java' for Java classes, and 'src' for source code.
Create a new layout file: Create a new file called 'activity_main.xml' in the 'layout' directory and add a text view to display the directory list.
Design the user interface: Use a layout editor to design the user interface, add a button to navigate to the next page, and a text view to display the directory list.
Create a new activity: Create a new activity class called 'MainActivity.java' in the 'java' directory and inherit from the AppCompatActivity class.
Define the activity layout: In the 'MainActivity.java' file, define the activity layout using the 'setContentView' method and inflate the 'activity_main.xml' layout.
Populate the directory list: In the 'MainActivity.java' file, use a RecyclerView to populate the directory list with the data from the 'DirectoryAdapter' class.
Create a new adapter class: Create a new adapter class called 'DirectoryAdapter.java' in the 'java' directory and extend the RecyclerView.Adapter class.
Define the adapter layout: In the 'DirectoryAdapter.java' file, define the adapter layout using the ' inflates' method and inflate the 'item_layout.xml' layout.
Populate the adapter list: In the 'DirectoryAdapter.java' file, use a binding to inflate the 'item_layout.xml' layout and bind the data to the adapter.
Create a new item layout: Create a new layout file called 'item_layout.xml' in the 'layout' directory and add a text view to display the directory name.
